differences.title: 1. Boilerplate
differences.leftDesc: Requires very little setup, straight to the point.
differences.rightDesc: Involves actions, reducers, and store configuration.

differences.title: 2. Learning Curve
differences.leftDesc: Easy to grasp and implement quickly.
differences.rightDesc: Requires understanding concepts like immutability and middleware.

differences.title: 3. Bundle Size
differences.leftDesc: Adds minimal overhead to your application's size.
differences.rightDesc: Includes more features, contributing to a larger footprint.

differences.title: 4. Dev Tools
differences.rightDesc: Rich debugging with time-travel and action inspection.

differences.title: 5. Immutability
differences.leftDesc: Mutations are allowed, managed by its own hooks.
differences.rightDesc: Requires strict adherence to immutable updates.

differences.title: 6. Middleware
differences.leftDesc: External libraries needed for async operations.
differences.rightDesc: Supports middleware for async actions and logging.

differences.title: 7. Use Case
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for projects needing simple global state management.
differences.rightDesc: Best for enterprise-level applications with complex state needs.
